economic_notes: Caldwell Settlement Trust with net assets of $94,630,674 (Dec 31 2022), originally funded by $75,000,000 Government of Canada contribution. Gas station operation commenced in fiscal 2023 generating $6,173,109 in revenue. Portfolio investment of $5,000,000 GIC at 3.95% with Peace Hills Trust. The Happy Snapper Inc. and Turtle Landing Inc. are government business enterprises accounted for on modified equity basis.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not yet determinable for buildings (possible asbestos removal costs); (2) auditors were not appointed for Northwind Business Development LP and could not verify inventory. Financial statements are for fiscal year ended March 31, 2023, not 2024. The Caldwell Settlement Trust ($94.6M net assets) is NOT consolidated — it is a separate trust with economic interest only. Gas station operations are new in fiscal 2023, contributing $6.17M revenue. 24 wholly-owned subsidiaries consolidated. 2 GBEs on modified equity basis. Expenses by segment: Health Care $2,185,804; Internally Developed $12,159,759; General $16,297,546. Total government transfers estimated as sum of ISC + AIAI + ONFLP + Ministry of Children + Ministry of Health + FNIHB + CMHC + Hydro One + Parks Canada + Environment Canada + Field Liaison + Ontario Trillium + Windsor-Detroit Bridge + FNFMB + Ministry of Indigenous Affairs + Canada Community Revitalization Fund. OSR includes investment/trust revenue, rent, gas station, crop sales, program admin fees, general revenue.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
